title: gavilea trullata
intro: Gavilea trullata, also known as the Toothed Gavilea, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is native to New Zealand and has been used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Gavilea trullata is often made into a tea or infusion, which is believed to provide health benefits such as immune system support and relief from respiratory issues.
